stat_of_week: 0 - Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs were shut out 9-0 by the Wisconsin Rapids Reapers, becoming the first team in league history to win zero categories in a single week
thriller_note: BaseballPro77 survived on the slimmest of margins despite a weak offensive showing (.277 OBP, only 2 HR), while the Benchwarmers out-hit and out-scored them in several categories but couldn't close it out — a result that keeps both teams hovering in the middle of the standings with little margin for error going forward.
key_storyline: The Sugar Land Skeeters continued their reign of terror with a dominant 8-1 dismantling of Hiroshima Radiation, scoring a league-high 40 runs on the week while Drake Baldwin, Matt Olson, and CJ Abrams all put up monster numbers. Over two weeks of cumulative category wins, the Skeeters sit atop the league and look like the clear class of the field. Meanwhile, the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs suffered one of the most embarrassing weeks in league memory, going 0-for-9 in categories against Wisconsin Rapids Reapers and posting a historically anemic offensive line — their roster construction is now a serious concern heading into Week 3.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers posted below-average numbers across nearly every pitching category and lost the strikeout and ERA battles badly, yet still nearly stole the matchup with a 4-5 result, reflecting the largest positive luck delta (+0.58) in the league this week.
unlucky_reason: General Hospital posted a sparkling 2.10 ERA, 1.02 WHIP, and solid offensive numbers good enough to dominate most opponents, yet their expected win total of 0.92 — the highest in the league — vastly exceeded their actual result, signaling they were matched up against a team that happened to edge them in just enough categories to steal the win.
records_broken: General Hospital ties their own league record with a 2.10 ERA in a winning week, matching the mark set in Week 2 (previous record: 2.10 ERA, General Hospital, Week 2) — technically no new record broken, but the Abbotsford-Colby Twins tied the all-time strikeout record with 77 K, matching the previous mark also set by Abbotsford-Colby Twins in Week 2.
